ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR ON OCT-23-2013 IN 503, DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS RULING - DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED. DEFENDANT SUSTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN. THE DECLARATION OF RUDY LIMPERT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT IS LIABLE FOR MR. ALBERIGI'S EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT'S AMMCO BRAKE GRINDING MACHINE. WHILE SUCH MACHINE ITSELF DID NOT CONTAIN ASBESTOS OR ASBESTOS-CONTAINING COMPONENT PARTS, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY TO MR. ALBERIGI. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATIONS OF CRAIG MOUNTZ AND RUSSELL DARNELL ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF RUDY LIMPERT ARE OVERRULED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its ruling - Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declaration of Rudy Limpert creates a triable issue whether defendant is liable for Mr. Alberigi's exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant's Ammco brake grinding machine. While such machine itself did not contain asbestos or asbestos-containing component parts, there is a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Alberigi. Plaintiffs' objections to the declarations of Craig Mountz and Russell Darnell are overruled. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Rudy Limpert are overruled. Prevailing party to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter Not Reported (503/TLJ)
